title: Recover the Heap After Deletion of Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown: Recover the heap after the deletion of duplicate elements. Given an array of integers, for each duplicate element in the array, remove one occurrence from the array. Then, build the heap from the modified array. Return the heap as an array of integers.
statement.inputFormat: An integer count followed by a line of integers separated by spaces. The integers are in the range of 1 to n
statement.outputFormat: The recovered heap as a line of integers separated by spaces
statement.notes: The input array may have duplicate elements.

examples.explanation: Input: [3, 5, 3, 7, 3]. Remove the duplicates and build the heap: [3, 5, 7].

hints.text: Start by removing duplicate elements from the array.

editorial.title: Recover the Heap After Deletion of Duplicate Elements
editorial.intuition: To solve this problem, we need to remove the duplicates from the array and then build the heap.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to remove duplicates and then build the heap.
